 {"id":"bd-ymg8v","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_readdir_image fixture leaves are missing tree_block_csum stamping","description":"# Review finding

`build_btrfs_readdir_image()` builds synthetic root-tree and fs-tree leaves for the readdir suite, but it never stamps `tree_block_csum` on either leaf.

## Evidence
- Builder: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23165-23311`
- It writes the root-tree leaf at `root_tree_logical = 0x4000` and the fs-tree leaf at `fs_tree_logical = 0x20000`.
- Unlike the fsops fixtures, this builder never invokes the existing checksum helper (`stamp_btrfs_test_tree_block_crc32c`), and it switches to `nodesize = 16384`, so both synthetic tree blocks must be stamped consistently with the parser’s current checksum expectations.
- The workspace rerun still fails all readdir-specific consumers with `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`:
  - `walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
  - `btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
  - `btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
  - `btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
  - `btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
  - `btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`

## Why this is high severity
This is a second stale synthetic-fixture family independent of the minimal open/root-tree fixture. It blocks all of the new btrfs directory-iteration regression coverage.

## Repro
`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Stamp checksums for both generated leaves after all items/payloads are finalized, with nodesize-aware coverage for the 16 KiB fs-tree case.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:05:03.818965727Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","close_reason":"Already shipped; builder already stamps both blocks and targeted btrfs_readdir repros now pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

 {"id":"bd-ynuv","title":"Add E2E FUSE mount integration tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T16:05:49.393331130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:38.263294368Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T16:39:38.263267789Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ynv2","title":"Enable default-run FUSE E2E smoke tests with runtime skip gating","description":"Unignore a minimal stable subset of ffs-harness FUSE E2E tests that already self-skip when prerequisites are missing, so capable hosts get coverage by default without failing restricted environments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T23:46:38.317832135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:38:42.937747106Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:38:42.937727900Z","close_reason":"Unignored fuse_getattr_file_metadata and fuse_readlink_and_symlink_detection — both are read-only smoke tests that already runtime-skip via fuse_available(). Now 5 tests run by default (read_hello_txt, readdir_root, read_nested_file, getattr_file_metadata, readlink_and_symlink_detection). Write-path and btrfs tests remain gated with #[ignore]. All gates pass (check, clippy, fmt).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-yrii","title":"JBD2 descriptor/revoke block checksum omits 4-byte tail instead of zeroing it","description":"In crates/ffs-journal/src/lib.rs, verify_jbd2_block_checksum handles JBD2_BLOCKTYPE_DESCRIPTOR and JBD2_BLOCKTYPE_REVOKE by computing crc32c over &block[..bs - 4]. However, Linux jbd2_descriptor_block_csum_verify temporarily zeroes the 4-byte tail checksum field and then computes the checksum over the *entire* block size (j->j_blocksize). By slicing off the last 4 bytes instead of hashing 4 zero bytes, the rust implementation produces a different CRC32C value and will incorrectly fail validation for valid JBD2 descriptor blocks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-18T19:34:41.195653951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:06:51.920432808Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:06:51.918348189Z","close_reason":"Descriptor/revoke checksum verification now hashes the full block with a zeroed 4-byte tail, matching Linux jbd2 semantics; added regression coverage and passed targeted rch fmt/test/check/clippy.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -152,6 +152,9 @@ drift.
 Mounted-path ext4 rename refusal coverage now also freezes exact `EISDIR` for
 file-over-directory overwrites and exact `ENOTDIR` for directory-over-file
 overwrites, with no root-entry, source-child, or file-byte drift.
+Mounted-path ext4 rename refusal coverage now also freezes exact `ENOTEMPTY`
+for rename-over-nonempty-directory attempts, with no root-entry, child-entry,
+or child-file drift after the rejected overwrite.
 Mounted-path btrfs rename coverage now also freezes same-name source/destination
 semantics as a visible no-op, with no directory-entry drift and no inode/data
 drift.
